top_stories.title: Entra passkey enrollment vishing targets Microsoft 365 users
top_stories.summary: A threat actor is conducting vishing (voice phishing) attacks targeting Microsoft 365 users by impersonating security personnel and requesting victims to enroll a new Microsoft Entra passkey. The campaign spans multiple sectors and abuses the Entra passkey enrollment process to gain unauthorized access to victim accounts.

top_stories.title: Hackers Use Fake Microsoft Entra Passkey Enrollment to Gain Microsoft 365 Access
top_stories.summary: A threat actor tracked as O-UNC-066 is conducting voice-based phishing attacks that trick Microsoft 365 users into enrolling a fraudulent Microsoft Entra passkey, enabling unauthorized access for data extortion. Okta researchers identified the campaign, which uses a panel-controlled phishing kit specifically designed to compromise the passkey enrollment process across multiple sectors.

top_stories.title: Forg365 phishing platform uses AI and device code flows to breach Microsoft 365
top_stories.summary: A phishing-as-a-service platform called Forg365 has emerged, specifically targeting Microsoft 365 accounts using AI-generated lures and device code flow abuse. The platform exploits OAuth device code authentication flows to bypass MFA protections and hijack user sessions without requiring credential theft.

top_stories.title: Extortion crew hijacks Microsoft 365 accounts via fake passkey setup
top_stories.summary: A cyber extortion group called Pink is conducting vishing attacks that trick employees into completing fake Microsoft Entra passkey enrollment requests, granting attackers access to Microsoft 365 accounts. The attack chain begins with a phone call from someone impersonating IT staff, leading victims through a fraudulent passkey setup process that hands over account access.

top_stories.title: Delinea Achieves FedRAMP High Authorization for Secret Server
top_stories.summary: Delinea has achieved FedRAMP High Authorization to Operate (ATO) for its PAM solution, Secret Server. This certification allows Delinea to serve U.S. federal agencies with the highest level of FedRAMP security clearance for privileged access management.

news.title: Hackers exploit critical auth bypass in Gitea Docker image
news.summary: A critical authentication bypass vulnerability in the official Gitea Docker image is being actively exploited by attackers. The flaw allows threat actors to impersonate any user, including administrators, on self-hosted Gitea instances.

news.title: The Replicant in Your Directory: AI Agents and the Identity Security Gap
news.summary: Netwrix published an analysis on the identity security challenges posed by AI agents and the proliferation of non-human identities in enterprise environments. The piece focuses on visibility gaps and identity governance requirements as AI-driven non-human identities expand the attack surface.

news.title: AWS gives its ERP agent deny-by-default rules and a separate identity
news.summary: AWS has released an ERP automation agent that operates with deny-by-default permission rules and a separate, isolated identity for performing accounts receivable and finance tasks. The agent's architecture applies least-privilege access principles and a distinct identity to the AI agent, directly touching IAM concepts like identity separation and default-deny authorization policies in agentic AI systems.

news.title: Only 28% of financial workforce MFA is phishing-resistant
news.summary: A Secret Double Octopus report finds that only 28% of MFA implementations in financial sector workforces are phishing-resistant, with password plus OTP remaining the most common authentication method. The report highlights that financial organizations rely on a mix of authentication methods, leaving many still vulnerable to phishing and credential theft.

news.title: AI Agents Are a New Kind of Identity — and Most Organizations Aren't Ready
news.summary: The article addresses the emerging challenge of managing AI agents as a distinct identity type within enterprise IAM frameworks. It argues that existing identity constructs like service accounts and API tokens are inadequate for governing AI agent identities, calling for a fundamentally different approach.

news.title: AI Gateways Offer Attackers the Keys to the Kingdom
news.summary: A cryptomining incident demonstrated how compromised AI gateways can expose IAM data alongside AI models and cloud infrastructure. The attack vector highlights how AI gateway deployments create new pathways for attackers to access identity and access management systems and credentials.

news.title: New Helix vishing group emerges in SharePoint data theft attacks
news.summary: A new threat actor group called Helix has emerged, using identity-focused attack techniques including vishing, device code phishing, and MFA abuse to steal data from SharePoint environments. The group's tactics specifically target authentication and identity mechanisms to gain unauthorized access and exfiltrate data.

news.title: New Forg365 phishing platform uses AI to target Microsoft 365 accounts
news.summary: A new phishing-as-a-service platform called Forg365 has emerged, targeting Microsoft 365 accounts using a combination of adversary-in-the-middle and device code phishing techniques alongside AI-generated lures. The platform directly attacks authentication mechanisms used in Microsoft 365, bypassing MFA protections through session token theft via AiTM proxying.

news.title: npm 12 Disables Install Scripts by Default to Reduce Supply Chain Risk
news.summary: GitHub released npm version 12 with install scripts disabled by default and deprecated granular access tokens (GATs) that could bypass two-factor authentication (2FA). The changes are opt-in, targeting supply chain risk reduction in the npm package ecosystem.

news.title: Introducing OAuth Support for AWS MCP Server
news.summary: AWS has introduced OAuth support for its MCP (Model Context Protocol) Server, enabling authentication using existing AWS credentials and sign-in methods. The implementation uses a browser-based experience powered by industry-standard OAuth, aligning with the same credentials used for AWS Management Console and AWS CLI access.

news.title: Palo Alto, CrowdStrike, and Cisco pour billions into solving AI’s credential sharing problem
news.summary: Palo Alto Networks, CrowdStrike, and Cisco are making substantial investments targeting AI's credential sharing problem. The investments focus on developing robust identity solutions to enable secure AI deployment and address machine/AI identity management challenges.

news.title: INVESTOR ALERT: Pomerantz Law Firm Investigates Claims On Behalf of Investors of SailPoint, Inc. - SAIL
news.summary: Pomerantz Law Firm has announced it is investigating claims on behalf of investors of SailPoint, Inc. (NASDAQ: SAIL). The investigation is being conducted by Pomerantz LLP, a firm specializing in securities class action lawsuits, with investors advised to contact the firm directly.

news.title: Microsoft launches Windows 365 for Agents to secure AI workloads
news.summary: Microsoft has launched Windows 365 for Agents, a Cloud PC service providing isolated virtual machine environments for running AI agents. The service allows organizations to apply security and compliance frameworks to AI workloads through managed, dedicated virtual machines.

news.title: Common MFA mistakes -- and how to fix them
news.summary: The article examines common mistakes organizations make when implementing MFA that allow attackers to bypass authentication controls. The focus is on improper configuration and management of MFA systems as the primary cause of successful circumvention attacks.

news.title: FusionAuth Launches Intelligent MFA in Latest Release as Demand Surges for Identity Infrastructure Customers can Control
news.summary: FusionAuth has launched an Intelligent MFA feature built on a 10-signal risk engine, enabling adaptive authentication in customer-controlled deployments. The release coincides with the company reporting 201% quarter-over-quarter bookings growth, indicating rising demand for its CIAM infrastructure.

news.title: 1Kosmos Recognized by Microsoft as Entra Verified ID Partner for Delivering Secure Remote Onboarding and Enhanced Digital Trust
news.summary: 1Kosmos has been recognized by Microsoft as an Entra Verified ID partner for delivering secure remote onboarding using verifiable credentials. The collaboration aims to reduce hiring fraud, synthetic identities, and unauthorized account creation through integration with Microsoft's decentralized identity platform.

news.title: Permiso brings FICO-style risk scores to human, machine and AI identities
news.summary: Permiso Security launched the Risk Score Engine, a model that assigns continuous, multidimensional risk scores to human, machine, and AI identities across organizational environments. The scoring approach is modeled after FICO-style credit scoring, providing unified identity risk assessment across all identity types on its security platform.

news.title: ProofID Appoints Neil Evans as Chief AI Officer to Lead Transformation into an AI-Native Identity Security Specialist
news.summary: ProofID, an identity security specialist, has appointed Neil Evans as its newly created Chief AI Officer role. The position is focused on embedding an AI-native approach across the business, blending commercial, technical, and people leadership.

news.title: Keeper Security surpasses $225M in ARR with transformative growth and is emerging as the market standard for AI-native identity security
news.summary: Keeper Security announced it has surpassed $225 million in annual recurring revenue, positioning itself toward a potential $1 billion ARR and public offering. The company attributes its growth to product-market fit in agentic AI security, expanding identity threats, and its password/privileged access management platform.

news.title: Lone Attacker Uses AI to Breach AWS Cloud Environment in 72 Hours
news.summary: A lone attacker breached an AWS cloud environment within 72 hours by exploiting AI workflows, chaining cloud misconfigurations, and using stolen credentials. The attack resulted in extortion of a large Amazon customer, highlighting credential theft and cloud access control failures as key vectors.

news.title: What's the best way to do authentication in modern applications
news.summary: The article addresses authentication token storage options for modern applications, targeting frontend developers. It compares different approaches to storing login tokens, presenting the trade-offs of each method.

news.title: Quest Software Strengthens Identity Security and Resilience for the AI Era, Achieving FedRAMP® High Authorized Certification for Identity Defense and Identity Recovery
news.summary: Quest Software has achieved FedRAMP High Authorized Certification for its Identity Defense and Identity Recovery solutions. This certification allows federal and regulated organizations to use Quest's identity security and resilience tools at the highest level of government-grade assurance.

news.title: SailPoint、「Agentic Acceleration」を発表、時間を要するクラウド モダナイゼーションを、迅速なシステム導入へと変革
news.summary: SailPoint announced a new offering called "Agentic Acceleration," aimed at transforming cloud modernization processes to enable faster system deployment. The announcement was originally made on June 16, 2026, with this being a Japanese-language translation of the US press release.

news.title: Big Brand Jobs Scam Targets Marketing Pros' Google Accounts
news.summary: A phishing campaign is targeting marketing professionals' Google accounts using nested redirects and credential theft tactics. The attack impersonates big brand job offers to lure victims into surrendering their credentials.

news.title: Keeper Security Extends Agentic AI Governance to Endpoint Privilege Manager
news.summary: Keeper Security has extended its agentic AI governance capabilities to its Endpoint Privilege Manager, enabling discovery and governance of AI agents on employee workstations and endpoints. The update introduces access policies and elevation controls for AI agents operating at the endpoint level, framed within Keeper's zero-trust and zero-knowledge identity security platform.

news.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
news.summary: Two members of the Scattered Spider cybercrime group pleaded guilty in the UK to charges related to the August 2024 cyberattack on Transport for London. Scattered Spider is known for sophisticated social engineering and identity-based attacks, including SIM swapping and MFA bypass techniques used to compromise enterprise environments.

news.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
news.summary: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support assistant chatbot to bypass account security and reset Instagram passwords, temporarily seizing high-profile accounts including the Obama White House and a U.S. Space Force official. Instructions for the social engineering technique were distributed via Telegram, enabling attackers to manipulate the AI bot into performing unauthorized account recovery actions.

news.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
news.summary: A CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and other agency secrets to a public GitHub account, resulting in a significant credential leak. Congressional lawmakers in both chambers are demanding answers from CISA as the agency works to contain the breach and invalidate the compromised credentials.
